name: Proton charge radius from muonic hydrogen and electronic spectroscopy
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the proton charge radius r_p measured by muonic-hydrogen Lamb-shift spectroscopy and (post-2017) by re-measured electronic-hydrogen spectroscopy: r_p = 0.84087 +/- 0.00039 fm (muonic H, Antognini et al. 2013), in agreement with later electronic-H measurements within ~0.001 fm. The earlier 'proton radius puzzle' (~5 sigma discrepancy 2010-2017) is now considered largely resolved.

regime.description: Sub-fm proton-charge structure; sensitive to any new electron-proton or muon-proton interaction.

current_bound.experiment: Muonic hydrogen Lamb-shift spectroscopy at PSI
current_bound.reference: Antognini et al., 'Proton structure from the measurement of 2S-2P transition frequencies of muonic hydrogen', Science 339, 417 (2013); Pohl et al., Nature 466, 213 (2010)

what_it_excludes: The post-2017 re-measurements of the electronic-hydrogen 2S-2P Lamb shift (Bezginov et al. 2019, Beyer et al. 2017) give r_p consistent with the muonic value, settling the proton-radius puzzle. This eliminates motivation for muon-specific new physics that would have been needed to explain a genuine muonic-vs-electronic difference, but tightly constrains any framework predicting flavor-non-universal electromagnetic couplings.

parameterization.name: Direct: proton charge radius r_p = (-6 dG_E/dQ^2 |_{Q^2=0})^(1/2). Bound-state QED corrections are calculable to alpha^7 m c^2 / h precision.
parameterization.reference: Antognini et al., Annals Phys. 331, 127 (2013); Pachucki, PRA 60, 3593 (1999).
parameterization.summary: Lamb shift in muonic H depends on r_p^2 with O(1) coefficient because the muon Bohr radius is ~200 times smaller, dramatically enhancing finite-size effects.

procedural.predicted_observable: 2S_1/2 - 2P_3/2 splitting in muonic hydrogen; 1S Lamb shift in electronic hydrogen.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: r_p (muonic H) = 0.84087 +/- 0.00039 fm; r_p (electronic, recent) consistent at ~0.001 fm. Older CODATA r_p = 0.8775 +/- 0.0051 fm (electronic H + e-p scattering, pre-2017) caused the original puzzle.
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts flavor-non-universal QED corrections, compute the resulting shift in r_p^muonic vs r_p^electronic.
If the framework introduces new short-range e-p or mu-p interactions, compute their contribution to the Lamb shift in each system.

primary_references.citation: Antognini et al., 'Proton structure from the measurement of 2S-2P transition frequencies of muonic hydrogen', Science 339, 417 (2013)
Pohl et al., 'The size of the proton', Nature 466, 213 (2010)
Beyer et al., 'The Rydberg constant and proton size from atomic hydrogen', Science 358, 79 (2017)
Bezginov et al., 'A measurement of the atomic hydrogen Lamb shift and the proton charge radius', Science 365, 1007 (2019)
